title: Model string requires provider prefix — no default from orchestration agent settings
description: parseModelSpec splits the model string on '/' to separate provider from modelId. Without a '/', the provider defaults to 'anthropic'. So 'deepseek-v4-pro' resolves to anthropic/deepseek-v4-pro — which doesn't exist. The correct format is 'deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro'. The orchestration agent's defaultProvider from settings.json is not consulted for dispatch. The model string format is not documented.
actual_behavior: model 'deepseek-v4-pro' → parseModelSpec → { provider: 'anthropic', modelId: 'deepseek-v4-pro' } → not found in modelRegistry. model 'kimi-k2.6:cloud' → { provider: 'anthropic', modelId: 'kimi-k2.6:cloud' } → not found. model 'deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro' → { provider: 'deepseek', modelId: 'deepseek-v4-pro' } → found, dispatch succeeds.
expected_behavior: Either: (a) parseModelSpec should fall back to the orchestration agent's defaultProvider from settings when no '/' is present, or (b) the provider/ prefix requirement should be documented in the AgentSpec tool description and the call-agent error message should suggest the correct format.
reproduction: 1. author-agent-spec with model 'deepseek-v4-pro'. 2. call-agent. 3. Observe 'not found in modelRegistry'. 4. Change to 'deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro'. 5. Dispatch succeeds.
workaround: Use provider/modelId format in the model field: 'deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro', 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514', 'ollama/kimi-k2.6:cloud'.

code_references.snippet: if (slashIndex !== -1) return { provider: spec.slice(0, slashIndex), modelId: spec.slice(slashIndex + 1) }; return { provider: 'anthropic', modelId: spec };
